curriculum.name: 2021 Primary Mathematics Syllabus
curriculum.scope_note: The PSLE assesses attainment at the end of primary education. Primary 6 uses the 2021 syllabus from 2026, so paper generation must include prerequisite content learned in earlier primary levels as well as the Primary 6 content below.

learning_outcomes: Divide a proper fraction by a whole number without a calculator.
Divide a whole number or proper fraction by a proper fraction without a calculator.
Find the whole when a part and the percentage are given.
Find percentage increase and percentage decrease.
Use, represent and interpret a:b and a:b:c where a, b and c are whole numbers, excluding ratios involving fractions and decimals.
Recognise and form equivalent ratios.
Divide a quantity in a given ratio.
Express a ratio in its simplest form.
Find the ratio of two or three given quantities.
Find a missing term in a pair of equivalent ratios.
Understand the relationship between fraction and ratio.
Use a letter to represent an unknown number.
Use, represent and interpret simple algebraic expressions involving addition, subtraction, multiplication and division.
Simplify simple linear expressions without brackets.
Evaluate simple linear expressions by substitution.
Solve simple linear equations involving whole-number coefficients only.
Find the area and circumference of a circle.
Find the area and perimeter of a semicircle and a quarter circle.
Find the area and perimeter of composite figures made from squares, rectangles, triangles, semicircles and quarter circles.
Find one dimension of a cuboid when its volume and the other dimensions are given.
Find the length of one edge of a cube when its volume is given.
Find the height of a cuboid when its volume and base area are given.
Find the area of a face of a cuboid when its volume and one dimension are given.
Use square and cube roots in the relevant volume problems.
Find unknown angles, without constructing additional lines, in composite figures involving squares, rectangles, triangles, parallelograms, rhombuses and trapeziums.

primary_6_content.topic: Average of a Set of Data
learning_outcomes: Understand average as total value divided by the number of data values.
Use the relationship between average, total value and number of data values.

assessment.purpose: Assess pupils' attainment in mathematics at the end of primary education with respect to the objectives of the Primary Mathematics syllabus.

objectives.description: Recall mathematical facts, concepts, rules and formulae, and perform straightforward computations and algebraic procedures.
Interpret information and understand and apply mathematical concepts and skills in a variety of contexts.
Reason mathematically, analyse information and make inferences, and select appropriate strategies to solve problems.

response_and_marking_rules.multiple_choice: Four options are provided and only one is correct.
response_and_marking_rules.short_answer: A question may have one or two parts. For a one-part 2-mark question, an incorrect answer may receive 1 mark for a correct method.
response_and_marking_rules.structured_or_long_answer: The candidate must show the method of solution clearly and write the answer in the space provided.
response_and_marking_rules.units: When a required unit is provided, the candidate gives the answer in that unit.
response_and_marking_rules.paperloop_ui_requirement: Keep the working canvas available for every non-multiple-choice item and capture it only when the student submits the typed final answer.
